headline: Defense Stocks Selloff Deepens. The Sector Can Survive a ‘Blue Wave.’
summary: The selloff in defense stocks has gotten out of hand, according to Citi analyst John Godyn.  Coming into Friday trading, shares of  Lockheed Martin Northrop Grumman General Dynamics RTX  and  L3Harris Technologies  were down 14% since the Iran war started and 8% since Monday.  “In just a few weeks, the top-down narrative on defense stocks has completely flip-flopped,” wrote Godyn on Thursday.

headline: Is L3Harris (LHX) Rebalancing Risk With Bigger Propulsion Bets and Steady Dividends?
summary: L3Harris Technologies has declared a quarterly cash dividend of US$1.25 per common share, payable on June 26, 2026, to shareholders of record as of June 5, 2026, while also advancing a more than US$1.00 billion expansion of its Virginia Advanced Propulsion Facilities to boost solid rocket motor capacity for Department of War programs. Together with the Department of War’s US$1.00 billion convertible preferred investment in its Missile Solutions business, these moves highlight L3Harris’s...
